t_ch: 未来是确定的，还是混沌的，这道难题依然无法解答。
t_en: Is the future destined or chaotic? This conundrum remains unsolvable.

t_ch: 真正的战争，从来都没有结束。
t_en: The true war has never ended.

t_ch: 情况紧急，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，请务必尽快出发。
t_en: The situation is urgent, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}, please depart immediately.

t_ch: 就这么定了——多来收集「权杖」的数据，保证你会看到不少刺激的好东西。
t_en: Then this settles it. Do come and collect more Scepter data. I guarantee that you'll see many exciting things.

t_ch: 这次的实验非同以往，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，还请注意安全。
t_en: This experiment is different from those prior, {F#ma'am}{M#sir} {NICKNAME}. Please stay safe.

t_ch: 「不可知域」…我已经迫不及待地想知道，还有什么在等着我们了。
t_en: The Unknowable Domain... I already can't wait to find out what awaits us.

t_ch: 与以往相同，我和斯蒂芬会在外部全力保障你们的安全。无论如何，请二位务必平安归来。
t_en: Just like before, Stephen and I will fully guarantee your safety outside. No matter what happens, you two must return safe and sound.

t_ch: 如果发现情况不对，马上往回跑，不用管我。还有，千万不要回头。
t_en: If you sense something amiss, turn around and run back with all you've got. Don't worry about me, and do NOT look behind you.

t_ch: 二世为自己打造的衣冠冢…以这一发现作为前提，结论：编写反转反有机方程的程序，其可行性并不为0。
t_en: The cenotaph the second emperor made for himself... With this discovery as the premise... Conclusion: The probability of composing a program that reverses the Anti-Organic Equation program is greater than 0.

t_ch: 未来是确定的，还是混沌的，这道难题依然无法解答。真正的战争，从来都没有结束。
t_en: Is the future destined or chaotic? This conundrum remains unsolvable. The true war has never ended.

t_ch: 关于「不可知域」…
t_en: About the Unknowable Domain...

t_ch: 有什么疑惑？
t_en: What kind of questions do you have?

t_ch: 我们要怎么解开「不可知域」？
t_en: How will we solve the Unknowable Domain?

t_ch: 收集「学派战争」的数据，复现出完整的「权杖」系统，到那时，鲁珀特二世的遗产自会告诉我们答案。
t_en: Collect data about the Scholars' Strife and replicate the complete Scepter system. Then, Rubert II's legacy will naturally show us the way.

t_ch: 波尔卡•卡卡目还会回来吗？
t_en: Will Polka Kakamond come back?

t_ch: 只要我还走在「智识」的命途上，想必总有一日会和她再度相遇的。只是不知道，下次见面她会准备什么样的手段来招呼我呢……
t_en: So long as I walk along the Path of Erudition, I think I'll end up meeting her again one day. I just don't know what she'll do to greet me next time...

t_ch: 不对劲…我怎么还有点小期待呢？
t_en: Something's off... Why am I looking forward to this?

t_ch: 我还有别的问题…
t_en: I still have some questions...

t_ch: 有人用某种锐器在黑塔一尘不染的模拟宇宙实验舱壁上刻下一行字——
t_en: Someone used a certain sharp object to carve a string of words on the wall of Herta's spotless Simulated Universe experiment pod...

t_ch: 「只有一种让我加入你们的方式：我的手术刀刺入了某位天才的咽喉。」
t_en: "There is only one way for me to join you all: When my scalpel pierces into the throat of a certain genius."

t_ch: 你明白，除你们之外，再也没有其他人踏入过此间，而那些人都不会是这行冰冷刻字的作者。
t_en: You understand that, besides you all, no one else has ever set foot in this space. And those people wouldn't be the authors of such a bleak declaration.

t_ch: 说起来，我还没怎么「开拓」过那节有吧台的车厢…今天难得悠闲，去逛逛吧。
t_en: Hmm, I haven't really "trailblazed" that bar car much, have I...? Heh, and I AM free today... Time to go check it out, then!

t_ch: 每次来这节车厢都忍不住感叹，真大呀……
t_en: Every time I come here, I can't help but marvel at just how spacious this car is...

t_ch: 怎么感觉面积都比其他车厢更宽敞，这符合物理学吗？
t_en: It also feels wider than the other cars... How is this even physically possible?

t_ch: 欢迎来到「派对车厢」，亲爱的无名客。您是要喝点饮料、吃点东西，还是要开始…「派对时刻」呢？
t_en: Welcome to the Party Car, Nameless. Would you like to have a drink, a snack, or... commence "Party Time?"

t_ch: 喝点东西吧。
t_en: I'll have a drink.

t_ch: 好的，请品尝这杯「柴米油盐」。它有浓稠的柴油质感，同时具备稻米的清香和海盐的清爽，是列车上享誉数个琥珀纪的饮品。
t_en: Right away. Here's a Rustic Infusion for you. The fragrant aroma of rice, the refreshing taste of sea salt, and a rich consistency matching that of oil — this has been a classic drink on board the Express for several Amber Eras now.

t_ch: 而且它的名字也颇具巧思——其中恰好暗藏了「柴油」二字，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: I particularly like the clever use of "rust" in its name. You can't help but laugh, can you?

t_ch: 吃点东西吧。
t_en: I'll have a snack.

t_ch: 好的，请品尝这道「扳手手拌沙拉」。它用料健康、分量刚好、味道清爽，正适合用来加餐。
t_en: Right away. Here's a Wrench Drenched Salad for you. Made with healthy ingredients in the perfect serving size, this is just the flavorful refreshment you should be reaching for between meals.

t_ch: 而且它的名字也颇具巧思——「扳手」与「手拌」形成了谐音与回文的精妙结构，而我的手恰似一对「扳手」，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: There's even a clever wordplay in its name: "Wrench" and "drenched" almost rhyme, and I have wrenches for hands! You can't help but laugh, can you?

t_ch: 「派对时刻」！
t_en: Party Time!

t_ch: 来临了，「派对时刻」！不过只有您一位无名客，恐怕是开不成的。还是下次吧。
t_en: It's Party Time! ...You're the only Nameless here. We can't start a party with only one person. Perhaps next time, then.

t_ch: 不过这让我想出一个笑话——有人问一位无名客：「你的派对来了几名客人？」那位无名客失落地回答道：「无名。」
t_en: This brings to mind a joke. Someone asked a Nameless: How many names are on your guest list? The Nameless shrugged and said, none, they were all "Nameless."

t_ch: 这车厢的名字好热闹啊…
t_en: This car has an upbeat name...

t_ch: 您是说「派对车厢」？的确，这是个热闹的名字。可惜现在的列车比较冷清，与「热闹」二字相去甚远。
t_en: You mean the Party Car? Yes, it does sound upbeat. It's a pity that the Express is so quiet right now... The exact opposite of "upbeat."

t_ch: 但这样也好。如果列车上人太多，我们的「派对车厢」恐怕就要变成「排队车厢」了。「派对」与「排队」谐音，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: But this might be for the best. If the train were too crowded, our "Party Car" might just become a "Panic Car." Another joke of mine! You can't help but laugh, can you?

t_ch: 我希望您能喜欢这节车厢，亲爱的无名客。就算开不成派对，您也可以在这里放松身心。
t_en: I hope you enjoy your time in the Party Car, dear Nameless. We may not be able to get a party started right now, but please feel free to relax and unwind here.

t_ch: 这节车厢以前叫「会议车厢」。我的{RUBY_B#虚空万藏}前任主人{RUBY_E#}说，现在无名客人这么少，用不到一整节车厢来开会，就自作主张改成了「派对车厢」。
t_en: This used to be called the Meeting Car, until my {RUBY_B#Void Archives}previous master{RUBY_E#} came along thinking they wouldn't need an entire car for meetings among just a handful of Nameless. That's when my previous master made the decision to rename it the Party Car.

t_ch: 话说，你是谁来着？
t_en: Actually... Who are you?

t_ch: 我只是一台调饮机器人，几个琥珀纪以来，致力于为各位无名客提供最优质的服务。
t_en: I'm merely a drinksmith robot who has been doing his best to provide impeccable service to the Nameless for several Amber Eras.

t_ch: 你是什么时候出现的…
t_en: Were you here all along...?

t_ch: 作为一台调饮机器人，我被设计为恰如其分地缺乏存在感。您不记得我，正符合设计预期。
t_en: As a robot drinksmith, my design ensures I remain appropriately inconspicuous. The fact that you don't remember me is precisely as intended.

t_ch: 需要我再为您做下自我介绍吗？
t_en: Should I introduce myself again?

t_ch: 三句话以内，谢谢。
t_en: Sure. No more than three sentences, please.

t_ch: 我是某位无名客发明的调饮机器人，于几个琥珀纪前被制造。后来被{RUBY_B#虚空万藏}前任主人{RUBY_E#}从角落里翻出，重新为星穹列车服务至今。
t_en: I am a robot drinksmith, invented and created several Amber Eras ago by a certain Nameless. My {RUBY_B#Void Archives}previous master{RUBY_E#} found me in a corner, dusted me off, and has had me serving passengers on the Astral Express ever since.

t_ch: 竟然两句话就介绍完了…那再补一句吧，我的名字「闭嘴」，来源于姬子小姐对前任主人说的最后一句话。
t_en: Oh, that only took two sentences. I'll add one more then. I was named after the last thing Miss Himeko said to my previous master: Shush.

t_ch: <unbreak>40</unbreak>字以内，谢谢。
t_en: Sure. Keep it under <unbreak>40</unbreak> words, please.

t_ch: 我是一台古老的调饮机器人，由{RUBY_B#虚空万藏}前任主人{RUBY_E#}修复。如今待在派对车厢，为各位调饮。
t_en: I am an antique robot drinksmith. My {RUBY_B#Void Archives}previous master{RUBY_E#} repaired me. I now work in the Party Car, mixing drinks for passengers.

t_ch: 晚些再来问你吧。
t_en: Maybe later.

t_ch: 好的，如您所愿。
t_en: Of course, as you wish.

t_ch: 顺带一提，三月七小姐和列车长刚刚去了楼上的房间，说要找您。
t_en: I should mention that the conductor and Miss March 7th went up to your room looking for you a short while ago.

t_ch: 我建议您上去看看他们，免得他们到处寻找。
t_en: Perhaps you should go and see them, to save them the hassle of looking all over the place for you.

t_ch: 来了来了，{NICKNAME}乘客，我们找你半天了！
t_en: There you are, {NICKNAME}! Pom-Pom has been looking for you all day!

t_ch: 你们怎么来这里找我？
t_en: What made you think I'd be here?

t_ch: 你睡昏头了吧？这里是你的房间，当然要到这里来找你了！
t_en: Are you daydreaming? This is your room, silly! Where else would we go to look for you, if not here?

t_ch: 我怎么有自己的房间了？
t_en: I have my own room now?

t_ch: 你是列车组的一员，当然有自己的房间了！
t_en: Of course you have one. You're part of the Crew!

t_ch: 这房间怎么这么乱啊…
t_en: Why is it such a mess in here...?

t_ch: 这应该问你自己……
t_en: Shouldn't you be asking yourself that...?

t_ch: 我失忆了！我是谁？你们是谁？
t_en: I've lost my memory! Who am I? Who are you guys?

t_ch: 嘿！本姑娘哐哐给你两拳，看你想不想得起来！
t_en: Hey, are you sure? Maybe a couple of punches will help to jog your memory?

t_ch: 哎，看着这一地杂物，就不由得想起了{NICKNAME}乘客刚成为无名客的时候……
t_en: Aw, looking at this mess reminds Pom-Pom of the time when {NICKNAME} first joined the Nameless...

t_ch: 是啊。这无比杂乱的储物间，将你的思绪带回了过去。
t_en: Indeed. This utterly cluttered storage room brings your thoughts back to the past.

t_ch: 那时，你刚刚在「黑塔」空间站经历了一场险象环生的冒险。然后你登上了星穹列车，展开了属于自己的太空喜剧。
t_en: Back then, you had just survived a perilous adventure on the Herta Space Station. Then, you boarded the Astral Express and began your own Space Comedy.

t_ch: 我记得，那好像是登上列车后不久的事……
t_en: I remember that. It was shortly after I boarded the Express...

t_ch: 不久…或很久以前
t_en: Some... or maybe a long time ago

t_ch: 彼时彼刻，刚登上列车时……
t_en: At that moment, when I first boarded the Express...

t_ch: 我…我就是听到通风管道里有声音，觉得藏了什么奇怪的东西，就想爬上去看一看，结果…就卡住了……
t_en: I... I'd heard noises coming from the ventilation ducts, which made me wonder if something was hiding in there. Curiosity got the better of me, and I climbed up to take a look... And then I got stuck...

t_ch: 那你找到「奇怪的东西」了吗？
t_en: So, did you find anything out of the ordinary in there?

t_ch: 找到了。是同样卡在里面的{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Mm-hmm. I found somebody else stuck in the ducts: {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你有什么想说的吗？
t_en: Any thoughts to share on the matter, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 我只是想探险。
t_en: I just wanted to explore the place.

t_ch: 真不错，是个「开拓」的好苗子。
t_en: I'm glad we found ourselves a natural at trailblazing.

t_ch: 请不要表扬{F#她}{M#他}帕！
t_en: Hmph. Don't go encouraging {F#her}{M#him}!

t_ch: 我有罪，对不起。
t_en: I apologize for my grave misjudgment.

t_ch: 那倒不至于。只是有些危险，下次不要这么做了。
t_en: Hehe, let's not go overboard. Still, it was dangerous — let's not do anything like that again.

t_ch: 我听到通风管道里有声音…
t_en: I heard noises coming from the ducts, so—

t_ch: 禁止套娃帕！
t_en: No copycat excuses!!!

t_ch: 好啦，今天是个好日子，就不做什么批评教育了。让各位齐聚一堂，是因为列车长有事要宣布。
t_en: Alright, alright. Today's a happy day, so Pom-Pom won't spoil it with lectures. I've gathered everyone here today because I have an announcement to make!

t_ch: 咳咳！让{NICKNAME}乘客在列车上如此居无定所，实在是本列车长的失职帕……
t_en: Ahem. It was terribly remiss of the conductor to have left {NICKNAME} without a room of {M#his}{F#her} own on the Express...

t_ch: 我看{NICKNAME}还挺乐在其中的？
t_en: {NICKNAME} seems pretty happy with the current arrangement, though!

t_ch: {M#他}{F#她}没有。
t_en: No, {F#she}{M#he} isn't.

t_ch: 哼哼，所以——姬子乘客，问出我们的问题帕！
t_en: Hehe, go on, Himeko. Ask the golden question!

t_ch: 我倒觉得，这些问题由列车长来问更合适哦。
t_en: Ah, but I think the conductor should be the one to do the honor instead.

t_ch: …咳咳…{NICKNAME}乘客，你是否愿意加入星穹列车，成为一名「无名客」？
t_en: ...Ahem... Then here goes: {NICKNAME}, will you join the Astral Express Crew as a Nameless?

t_ch: 我以为我早就是了。
t_en: I thought I was already part of the Crew.

t_ch: 哪有那么简单？你还没经过「杨叔十八机兵阵」的试炼，怎么称得上是合格的无名客呢？
t_en: Hehe, not so easy! You can't call yourself a Nameless until you've cleared the "<unbreak>18</unbreak> Sentinels: Mr. Yang" trial!

t_ch: 正好小三月也没经过试炼，我稍后去准备一下，明天下午就让你先挑战一次这「杨叔十八机兵阵」。
t_en: Which reminds me — March hasn't cleared it yet either. I'll set it up a little later — tomorrow afternoon you can be the first one to give it a shot.

t_ch: 别别别，小三月不懂事，说着玩的！
t_en: Ohhh! Please don't! I was just joking!

t_ch: 当然愿意了。
t_en: I'd love to.

t_ch: 哇，答应得好爽快！
t_en: Wow, you answered in a heartbeat!

t_ch: 可以拒绝吗？
t_en: Can I pass?

t_ch: 当然可以啦～但我肯定还是不希望你拒绝。
t_en: Of course! It's your choice, after all. But I'd really, really, really love for you to try!

t_ch: 还以为是要给我分配房间…
t_en: And here I thought you were going to assign me a room...

t_ch: 请{NICKNAME}乘客不要着急，那是下一个流程！
t_en: Don't jump the gun, {NICKNAME}! That's next on the list!

t_ch: 不必着急给出答案。
t_en: You don't have to give us an answer right away.

t_ch: 成为无名客，意味着你不再留恋过去，而是选择开拓未来。
t_en: Becoming a Nameless signifies leaving the past behind to blaze a trail into the future.

t_ch: 比起关照自身，你更爱探索世界。
t_en: Exploring the far corners of the universe will seem more exciting than just looking out for yourself.

t_ch: 要暂别熟悉的生活，前往未知的世界展开冒险。
t_en: You'll step out of your comfort zone to set forth on adventures into uncharted territories.

t_ch: 不再渴念脚下的大地，而是将远方的群星当做自己的故乡。
t_en: Your home will no longer be the places you know and love, but the distant stars.

t_ch: 以及最重要的——成为无名客，意味着往后你承担的职责远大于收获。
t_en: Above all else... Becoming a Nameless means the responsibilities you'll bear far outweigh the rewards you'll reap.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，即使如此，你也要成为无名客吗？
t_en: Now that you know all of this, {NICKNAME}, do you still wish to become a Nameless?

t_ch: 成为无名客
t_en: Yes, I do.

t_ch: 不成为无名客
t_en: No, do not.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，既然你决定成为…什么？！你竟然拒绝了！好吧…那，{NICKNAME}乘客，如果不做无名客，你想做什么？
t_en: Great! Since you've decided to become... Wait, what!? Pom-Pom can't believe you said no! Okay, fine... Then, if you don't want to be a Nameless, what do you want to do, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 加入巡海游侠，以武证道！
t_en: A Galaxy Ranger who upholds justice with their fists!

t_ch: 挥拳，挥拳，挥拳。直至欢呼和喝彩逐渐变为敬畏和忌惮，最后一个足以称为「敌人」的存在也在你面前倒下。在聚光灯下高举双拳的刹那，你一跃成为武学宗师，顿悟此中真谛。
t_en: A jab, a cross, and then a left hook! With nary a pause, the fists fly till the joyous cheers turn into silent awe and respect. Till the last worthy adversary falls before you. Raising your victorious fists in the spotlight, you welcome the epiphanic moment where you finally grasp the true meaning of martial arts and emerge as a grandmaster.

t_ch: 全灭，就是和平。
t_en: Now that they have all fallen, the world shall know peace.

t_ch: 全剧终
t_en: The End

t_ch: 停停停，别做白日梦了！请你好好选择！
t_en: Enough, enough! Stop daydreaming and start thinking about your answer!

t_ch: 加入星际和平公司，升职加薪！
t_en: A top-level executive of the IPC with a high salary!

t_ch: 手捧一杯十份浓缩的冷萃咖啡，你隔着玻璃注视着庇尔波因特的黎明。
t_en: Swirling a ten-shot cold brew espresso, you watch the sunrise upon Pier Point through the window.

t_ch: 从五千平米的办公室遥遥下望，数不尽的职员站在纵横交织的自动扶梯上，为了金钱、名声和即将到来的绩效评定奔波。
t_en: Looking down from your <unbreak>50,000</unbreak>-square-foot office, you see an endless stream of workers bustling up and down the extensive web of escalators, all of them slogging away to earn riches, to win prestige, and to nail that upcoming performance review.

t_ch: 他们的困扰、渴望、一个又一个步履难停的日夜，远不如你报表上的一个数字更能令你共情。
t_en: Their hardships, their ambitions, their ceaseless daily grind... None of that even means half as much to you as the figures on your report.

t_ch: 你嗤笑一声：你与他们之间的差别，远比他们与呜呜伯之间的差别更大。
t_en: You scoff: The difference between you and them is far greater than the difference between them and Wubbaboos.

t_ch: 全剧终
t_en: The End

t_ch: 停停停，别做白日梦了！请你好好选择！
t_en: Enough, enough! Stop daydreaming and start thinking about your answer!

t_ch: 成为谜语人，巴比巴伯巴拉！
t_en: A Riddler like Babbibalo Bala!

t_ch: 要制作最符合后现代表象本体论的不锈钢汉堡，需要兼顾基础矩阵的离散变量，并应用主动土压力对丙烯涂料进行能指剥离。
t_en: To create a stainless steel burger that best fits the apparent ontology of postmodernity, one must take into consideration the discrete variables in the fundamental matrices and apply active earth pressure in the separation and extraction of signifiers from acrylic paint.

t_ch: 填写相邻建筑物的离婚申请书时，应当遵循集合构建式符号的电子线折回，对齐颗粒度后熔断自我。
t_en: When filling out divorce papers for two adjacent buildings, we ought to adhere to the electrical cable foldback in the structural symbol set and align ourselves with their granularity before enabling the circuit breaker on ourselves.

t_ch: 全剧终
t_en: The End

t_ch: 停停停，别做白日梦了！请你好好选择！
t_en: Enough, enough! Stop daydreaming and start thinking about your answer!

t_ch: 好耶！我们以后一定能成为最好的开拓伙伴！
t_en: Yay! I'm sure we'll be the bestest Trailblazing buddies!

t_ch: 既然{NICKNAME}已经做出决定了，那就请各位围成一圈，准备开始新成员加入星穹列车的仪式帕！
t_en: Since {NICKNAME} has made {M#his}{F#her} decision, let's gather 'round and formally welcome the Astral Express Crew's newest member!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，准备好接受成为无名客的仪式了吗？
t_en: Are you ready for your initiation into the Nameless, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 早就准备好了！
t_en: You bet I am!

t_ch: 年轻人就是有精神，真羡慕。
t_en: You youngsters are so full of energy. I'm jealous.

t_ch: 不会要歃血为盟吧？
t_en: This doesn't involve a blood oath, does it?

t_ch: 不会啦～只是大家一起宣誓而已，很简单的。
t_en: Of course not! We're just gonna to recite a simple oath together.

t_ch: 不会要立投名状吧？
t_en: Do I need to do something to prove my loyalty?

t_ch: 没错，我当时就不得不亲手碾碎了一个…怎么可能啦，又不是犯罪团伙！
t_en: Yep! When I joined, I had to crush a... Just kidding! Why would you even think that? Haha! We're not a crime syndicate!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，请与我一起背诵开拓者的信条。
t_en: Let's recite the Trailblazer Creed. Now, {NICKNAME}, please repeat after Pom-Pom:

t_ch: （复诵信条）
t_en: (Recite creed.)

t_ch: 一，即使命途兴衰消长，开拓者应自有主张。二，即使面对惊涛骇浪，列车组应一致同向。
t_en: One, no matter the ebb and flow of fate, stop staying within the lines. Two, even in the face of tempestuous seas, we always align.

t_ch: 三，即使身处进退存亡，仍应与不义相抗。四，即使遭到世人遗忘，仍不计事后短长。
t_en: Three, even if we don't gain the upper hand, we'll fight for right. Four, even if forgotten by the world, never care a rap for hindsight.

t_ch: 五，即使银河暮色苍茫，仍应将长夜照亮。六，即使局面纷乱无章，仍应看向前方、碾碎乱象。
t_en: Five, no matter how bleak the cosmos may get, let us light the night. Six, even when there are wheels within wheels, go ahead!

t_ch: 愿此行，终抵群星。
t_en: May this journey lead us starward.

t_ch: 哎呀，这真的有点羞耻，要不下次取消这个环节吧？
t_en: Uhh, this is kind of cringey... You think we can drop this part of the initiation next time?

t_ch: 三月七乘客，请你不要破坏气氛帕！
t_en: Hey, March 7th! Don't ruin the moment!

t_ch: 咳咳…{NICKNAME}乘客，欢迎你正式成为「无名客」！
t_en: Ahem! You are now officially a Nameless, {NICKNAME}. Welcome to the Crew!

t_ch: 这太羞耻了，我不要…
t_en: This is kind of cringey. Can I skip over this part?

t_ch: 这…哪里羞耻了！仪式是很重要的帕！
t_en: H—How is it cringey!? This is an especially meaningful part of the initiation!

t_ch: 看吧！我就说大家都会觉得羞耻！
t_en: See! There's no way anyone can do this without feeling awkward!

t_ch: 这个环节以后就取消吧。
t_en: Alright alright. We'll leave this part out from now on.

t_ch: 怎、怎么这样！这可是开拓者的老规矩，祖宗之法不能变的帕！
t_en: You can't do that! Th—This is a time-honored tradition of the Trailblazers! You can't just change old customs as you please!

t_ch: 嗯，取消吧。
t_en: Yes, let's leave it out from now on.

t_ch: 对不起了列车长，看来我们两个是少数派。
t_en: I'm sorry, conductor, but it seems we're in the minority.

t_ch: 好吧，列车长只能尊重表决结果了帕…但不管怎么说！{NICKNAME}乘客，欢迎你成为「无名客」！
t_en: Well, if you all are absolutely certain... As the conductor, Pom-Pom can only respect the results of the vote. In any case, welcome, {NICKNAME}, our newest member of the Nameless!

t_ch: 那么回到最初的话题…{NICKNAME}，身为星穹列车的无名客，你应当拥有自己的房间。
t_en: Alright, let's get back on track. Now that you're a Nameless on the Astral Express, {NICKNAME}, it's only right that you have a room of your own.

t_ch: 没错！{NICKNAME}乘客终于不再居无定所了，帕姆好欣慰！
t_en: That's right! Pom-Pom is so glad that {NICKNAME} finally has a place of {M#his}{F#her} own!

t_ch: 只不过，如今的列车，车厢数量远比不上过去。「客房车厢」只有四间房间。严格来说，三间。丹恒甚至睡在资料室。
t_en: It's just that the current Express has far fewer cars compared to the past. The Passenger Cabin only has four rooms — well, three, technically, since Dan Heng has been sleeping in the Archives.

t_ch: 我倒不介意。
t_en: It doesn't bother me, though.

t_ch: 住观景车厢也不错！
t_en: Staying in the Parlor Car doesn't sound half bad!

t_ch: 观景车厢还要用来接待客人呢！
t_en: Don't even think about it! The Parlor Car is for receiving guests!

t_ch: 我们再买一节车厢吧！
t_en: Let's buy another car!

t_ch: 搭建车厢的配件可没那么容易买到帕……
t_en: It's not that easy to find the parts needed to build another car...

t_ch: 我和谁挤挤就好！
t_en: I can just room with one of you!

t_ch: 好，那你就和列车引擎挤挤帕～
t_en: Okay, you can room with the Express' engine, then!

t_ch: 不过「{NICKNAME}的房间」未必要在客房车厢。
t_en: {NICKNAME}'s room doesn't have to be in the Passenger Cabin, actually.

t_ch: 没错！在「派对车厢」二楼恰好有个闲置房间，可以当做{NICKNAME}的房间～走，我们快去看看吧！
t_en: He's right! There's an unused room on the second level of the Party Car that you can move into, {NICKNAME}. Let's go over and check it out!

t_ch: 在「派对车厢」二楼恰好有个闲置房间，可以当做{NICKNAME}的房间～走，我们快去看看吧！
t_en: There's an unused room on the second level of the Party Car that you can move into, {NICKNAME}. Let's go over and check it out!

t_ch: 欢迎，各位亲爱的无名客。嗯？看诸位满面春风，难道是发生了什么好事？莫非，「派对时刻」终于要到来了？
t_en: Greetings, my Nameless friends. My, look at those sunny smiles! Has something good happened? Don't tell me — is it finally Party Time?

t_ch: 或许，您会需要一个笑话作为派对开场白？来听听这个吧——「纯美骑士团」被「纯美」歧视了！「歧视」与「骑士」谐音，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: How about a joke to kick off the party? I have just the one for you: Behold, a Knight of Beauty on this night of beauty! Knight and night sound the same, get it? Ah, homophonous puns. You can't help but laugh, can you?

t_ch: 闭嘴，谢谢。…也许让它管理这节车厢前，应该先格式化一遍。
t_en: Shush. Thank you. Maybe we should reformat its system before allowing it to manage the car.

t_ch: 请注意，格式化设备将清除用户餐饮习惯、热量规划、代谢效率等所有数据！
t_en: Warning: Reformatting me will wipe all user data, including their eating habits, energy expenditure plans, metabolic rates, and more!

t_ch: 格式化期间不当操作，可能引起严重后果！包括但不限于维生系统紊乱、列车脱轨。佚失的「餐饮数据」，甚至可能引发一场新的「果核之灾」。
t_en: Mishandling during the reformatting process may also lead to serious consequences, including but not limited to disruption of the life support system and derailment of the Express. The loss of dietary data may even result in a new Subsistellaron Disaster!

t_ch: 当然，这些都只是派对前的小小玩笑。
t_en: I'm sure you've already realized, but that was just a joke for before the party begins.

t_ch: 瞧吧，又开始了。
t_en: Here we go again.

t_ch: 其实我还想听它讲笑话。
t_en: I haven't had enough of its jokes, actually.

t_ch: 噗，快再讲几个冷笑话～
t_en: Ahaha, keep the dad jokes coming!

t_ch: 等你们独处时再听他讲吧。我实在受不了这个。
t_en: ...Perhaps another time when I'm not here? Jokes like this make my head hurt, to put it mildly.

t_ch: 我就是为派对而生的！
t_en: I was born for parties!

t_ch: 来临了——「派对时刻」！
t_en: It's Party Time!

t_ch: 现在还不是开派对的时候吧！
t_en: I don't think now's the right time for a party!

t_ch: 点击格式化！
t_en: Select "Reformat"!

t_ch: 衷心感谢与您同行的这段时光，并祝您未来旅途愉快。请继续操作。
t_en: Thank you for all the time we've spent together. May your journey hereafter be a pleasant one. You may now resume reformatting.

t_ch: 点击「确认终止格式化」
t_en: Select "Abort Reformat"

t_ch: 感谢您选择「终止格式化」！我将继续竭诚为您服务。
t_en: Thank you for aborting the reformat operation! Rest assured I shall continue to serve the crew diligently.

t_ch: 根本就没得选嘛！
t_en: Why is there only one option!?

t_ch: 点击「取消格式化进程」
t_en: Select "Cancel Reformat"

t_ch: 感谢您选择「取消格式化」！我将继续竭诚为您服务。
t_en: Thank you for canceling the reformat operation! Rest assured I shall continue to serve the crew diligently.

t_ch: 根本就没得选嘛！
t_en: Why is there only one option!?

t_ch: 吵吵嚷嚷的，倒让帕姆想起了以前的日子。这节车厢还被称作「会议车厢」的时候，乘客们就是在这里，闹哄哄地表决下一个目的地。
t_en: Hehe, all this excitement really takes Pom-Pom back to the old days! When this car was still known as the "Meeting Car," the passengers would gather here and eagerly debate over their next destination.

t_ch: 这节车厢楼上，有个面积很大的储物间，现在基本处于闲置状态。作为房间还挺合适的。
t_en: There's a large storeroom that is not in use on the upper level of this car. I think it'll make a fitting room.

t_ch: 我住杂物间？真的假的？
t_en: You want me to live in a storeroom? Really?

t_ch: 会有猫头鹰给我送公司的录取通知书吗？
t_en: Is an owl going to bring me my offer letter from the IPC?

t_ch: 有帕姆给你送星穹列车的车票，还不知足嘛？别抱怨啦！整个列车就属你的房间最大！
t_en: Hmph, isn't the Astral Express boarding pass from Pom-Pom enough? You already have the biggest room on the Express, so just be happy with what you've got!

t_ch: 原本的杂物不要了？
t_en: What about the junk in there?

t_ch: 列车长囤积了很多「未来一定用得上」的东西，也是时候断舍离了。
t_en: The conductor has amassed a mountain of knickknacks over the years, thinking that they'll eventually be useful. We're overdue for some decluttering anyway.

t_ch: 别担心，依我看要不了多久，{NICKNAME}的零零碎碎就能把整个房间填满。
t_en: Hehe, but I bet it won't take long for {NICKNAME} to fill this room up with your own clutter.

t_ch: 这么多人一起上去，恐怕没处落脚。三月，和列车长陪{NICKNAME}上去看看新房间吧。
t_en: There won't be enough space to fit all of us. March, you and the conductor should go with {NICKNAME} to see {M#his}{F#her} new room.

t_ch: 走！带你看看去！
t_en: Okay! It's this way. Follow me!

t_ch: 这么多人一起上去，恐怕没处落脚。三月，和列车长陪{NICKNAME}上去看看新房间吧。
t_en: There won't be enough space to fit all of us. March, you and the conductor should go with {NICKNAME} to see {M#his}{F#her} new room.

t_ch: 走！带你看看去！
t_en: Okay! It's this way. Follow me!

t_ch: 咚咚…就是这里了帕！
t_en: Ta-da! We're here!

t_ch: 我就说这是最大的房间吧，都能关进一头末日兽了！
t_en: See? I wasn't kidding when I said this is the largest room on the Express! You could fit even a Doomsday Beast in here!

t_ch: 比我想象中还要大。
t_en: It's larger than I imagined.

t_ch: 是吧！再来两个末日兽也关得下！
t_en: Right? Even if you put a couple Doomsday Beasts in here, you'd still have room to spare!

t_ch: 希望你们不要有养这种宠物的歪心思。
t_en: Pom-Pom hopes you aren't actually thinking about keeping those things as pets...

t_ch: 再怎么大也就是个杂物间…
t_en: It's big, but it's still a storeroom...

t_ch: 哎呀，只要面积大，发挥空间就大～
t_en: Think of it this way: The more space there is, the more room you'll have to express your creativity!

t_ch: 卧榻之侧岂容末日兽鼾睡？
t_en: How could I allow a Doomsday Beast to slumber next to me?

t_ch: 没关系，就算是末日兽，现在也只能睡纸箱子。
t_en: Oh, that'd be the least of your worries. Even if a Doomsday Beast were here, it'd have to sleep in a cardboard box for now.

t_ch: 希望你们不要有养这种宠物的歪心思。
t_en: I hope you aren't actually thinking about keeping those things as pets...

t_ch: 虽然这个房间现在确实…呃…略显杂乱，但大家刚来的时候，房间差不多都是这个样子啦～
t_en: The room is... admittedly kind of messy right now, but so was everybody else's when they first boarded the Express.

t_ch: 别看我房间那么精致，那都是我自己花心思设计的！
t_en: My room may look pretty fancy now, but that's because I put a lot of effort into designing it myself!

t_ch: 因为一些「生活必需品」，三月七乘客的「列车基金」严重超支了哦。
t_en: Ahem! May I remind you that your expenses on "lifestyle essentials" have grossly exceeded your Express Fund budget?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，地毯、摇椅、抱枕、巨大帕姆玩偶…这些怎么不算生活必需品呢？
t_en: Carpets, pillows, rocking chairs, and jumbo Pom-Pom toys are lifestyle essentials, aren't they? Hehe.

t_ch: 二十多个枕头也是生活必需品吗？
t_en: Do you really need over <unbreak>20</unbreak> pillows, though?

t_ch: 当然了！不然要怎么打枕头大战呢？
t_en: Of course I do! How else would I have pillow fights?

t_ch: 总之，为了不再重蹈覆辙，我必须重申一下「列车基金」的调用规则！
t_en: In any case, to prevent such situations from happening again, I must reiterate the rules for accessing the Express Fund!

t_ch: 首先，列车长负责统一调遣列车内的物资，需要装修，就来找我帕。
t_en: Firstly, the conductor is in charge of all resource allocation within the Express. If you need any renovation work done, look for Pom-Pom!

t_ch: 其次，既然成为了星穹列车的正式乘员，就要承担起相应义务。要做值日，才能调用相应数额的「列车基金」，向我换取物资。
t_en: Secondly, since you're now officially a member of the Astral Express, you must be responsible for your share of duties aboard. Only then can you exchange the corresponding amount of Express Funds for supplies from Pom-Pom.

t_ch: 不想睡纸箱子，就暂且以「搭建卧室」为目标吧！
t_en: Unless you want to sleep in a cardboard box, I suggest prioritizing the renovation of your bedroom first.

t_ch: 诶？等会儿，帕姆给{NICKNAME}装修资金，再付给帕姆装修房子…好绕啊，列车长直接给{F#她}{M#他}装修不就好了！
t_en: Wait a second. Pom-Pom will provide {NICKNAME} with the renovation funds, then {NICKNAME} will use those funds to pay Pom-Pom for the renovations... What's the point of that? Just get the conductor to renovate {F#her}{M#his} room directly!

t_ch: 这叫程序正义帕！
t_en: This is called procedural justice!

t_ch: 无论如何，我现在是星穹列车的一员了，还有了自己的房间。
t_en: At any rate, I'm now a member of the Astral Express crew, and I even have my own room to boot.

t_ch: 今天就先将就一下吧。明天我一定好好整理，给自己搭建一个温暖的卧室……
t_en: I'll just make do for today. Tomorrow, I'll clean the place up and build myself a nice, cozy bedroom...

t_ch: 不久…或很久之后
t_en: Soon... or much later

t_ch: 此时此刻，依旧一片狼藉的毛坯房中……
t_en: At this moment, in the still messy unfurnished room...

t_ch: 你这拖延症算是没救了！
t_en: Jeez, you sure do love to procrastinate.

t_ch: 我其实明天就打算收拾的…
t_en: I was planning to start cleaning up tomorrow...

t_ch: 这都多少个明天了！
t_en: And how many times have you said that already!?

t_ch: 我都忘了自己有房间了。
t_en: I forgot that I have my own room now.

t_ch: 我只想问一个问题——睡纸箱子不硌得慌吗？
t_en: All I want to know now is... Isn't it uncomfortable to sleep on a cardboard box?

t_ch: 其实我只是想逃避值日。
t_en: Actually, I just wanted to avoid doing my duties.

t_ch: 我就知道是这样帕！
t_en: Heh, Pom-Pom knew it!

t_ch: 列车长也理解，万事开头难。
t_en: But Pom-Pom also knows that the first step is always the hardest.

t_ch: 今天我们就是来帮你解决开头问题的！
t_en: That is why we're here today — to help you with taking that first step!

t_ch: 帕姆准备了一笔启动款项。先试试用它建设卧室吧！
t_en: Pom-Pom has prepared a starter fund to help you kick-start the renovation. You can use it to set up your bedroom!

t_ch: 谢谢列车长！
t_en: Thank you, conductor!

t_ch: 用行动来表示感谢吧！今天我们陪你一起，无论如何都要先把卧室装修出来！
t_en: Hehe, appreciation is better shown with actions. We're getting that bedroom done one way or another!

t_ch: 好累，明天再说吧。
t_en: I'm tired. Let's discuss this again tomorrow.

t_ch: 不行！今天我们陪你一起，无论如何都要先把卧室装修出来！
t_en: Absolutely not! We're getting that bedroom done one way or another!

t_ch: 那就开始建设吧！
t_en: Let's get to it, then!

t_ch: 好耶！让我们行动起来吧！
t_en: Yay! Let's do this!

t_ch: 稍等，现在还有别的事。
t_en: Hold on. I've got something else I need to do.

t_ch: 快去快回哦，我们在这里等你帕。
t_en: Come back soon. We'll be waiting for you here.

t_ch: 今天我们陪你一起，无论如何都要先把卧室装修出来！
t_en: With us around, we're getting that bedroom done one way or another!

t_ch: 那就开始建设吧！
t_en: Let's get to it, then!

t_ch: 好耶！让我们行动起来吧！
t_en: Yay! Let's do this!

t_ch: 稍等，现在还有别的事。
t_en: Hold on. I've got something else I need to do.

t_ch: 快去快回哦，我们在这里等你帕。
t_en: Come back soon. We'll be waiting for you here.

t_ch: 呼呼，这样就大功告成啦！{NICKNAME}乘客的房间建设，终于迈出了第一步！
t_en: Phew! We're finally done! {NICKNAME} has finally taken {M#his}{F#her} first step toward completing the renovation of {M#his}{F#her} bedroom!

t_ch: 真是好感动帕…列车长还以为，没机会看到这间屋子被装修完成了……
t_en: Pom-Pom is so glad... I was worried I might never see this room get completed...

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这是{NICKNAME}的一小步，却是人类战胜拖延症的一大步！
t_en: Hahaha! One small step for {NICKNAME}, one giant leap toward humankind overcoming procrastination!

t_ch: 嗯…好像还少个枕头？虽然能睡纸箱子的人可能也不需要枕头……
t_en: Uh, we're still lacking a pillow in here. Although someone who can sleep comfortably on a cardboard box probably doesn't need one...

t_ch: 唔…不然先将就一下，明天再采购？
t_en: Hm... Let's just leave it for now, and get one tomorrow?

t_ch: 那可不行！只要拖到明天，就又会无限期拖延下去的！你们稍等我一下～
t_en: No can do! If we drag this out till tomorrow, it'll never be done! Wait here. I'll be right back!

t_ch: 三月七匆匆跑开了。几分钟之后，她捧着一个枕头敲开了房门。
t_en: March 7th hurriedly ran off. A few minutes later, she knocks the door open, holding a pillow in her arms.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，先用这个枕头睡觉吧～还好我的房间有好多枕头！我就说这是生活必需品吧～
t_en: Here, use this pillow for now, {NICKNAME}. It's a good thing that there's no shortage of pillows in my room! I told you pillows were essentials!

t_ch: 喔，不错帕～{NICKNAME}乘客，快把这个枕头放到床上吧。
t_en: Ho-ho, not bad! {NICKNAME}, hurry and place the pillow on your bed.

t_ch: （放下枕头）
t_en: (Put the pillow down)

t_ch: 让我鉴定一下，这枕头和你的卧室搭不搭～
t_en: Time for me to see if this pillow matches your bedroom!

t_ch: 还有事，稍等一下。
t_en: Give me a minute. I've got something else I need to do.

t_ch: 不是吧？这你都要拖延？
t_en: Are you serious? You're putting off even something like this?

t_ch: 喔，不错帕～{NICKNAME}乘客，快把这个枕头放到床上吧。
t_en: Ho-ho, not bad! {NICKNAME}, hurry and place the pillow on your bed.

t_ch: （放下枕头）
t_en: (Put the pillow down)

t_ch: 让我鉴定一下，这枕头和你的卧室搭不搭～
t_en: Time for me to see if this pillow matches your bedroom!

t_ch: 还有事，稍等一下。
t_en: Give me a minute. I've got something else I need to do.

t_ch: 不是吧？这你都要拖延？
t_en: Are you serious? You're putting off even something like this?

t_ch: 辛苦啦，{NICKNAME}乘客。今天早点休息吧！
t_en: You've done well today, {NICKNAME}. Get an early rest tonight!

t_ch: 明天可还有很多值日工作要做哦！嘿嘿，这次你可逃不掉啦～
t_en: There's a ton of duties to do tomorrow! Hehe, you won't slip away this time~

t_ch: 已经很晚了，我们就先告辞了帕！
t_en: It's getting late, so we'll see you later!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，晚安好梦哦～
t_en: Sweet dreams, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 陌生的天花板。
t_en: A strange ceiling.

t_ch: 但你隐约感到，它今后会成为你最熟悉的天花板。
t_en: But you have a vague feeling that it will become the ceiling you know best in the future.

t_ch: 明早你将看着它醒来，明晚你将看着它入睡…这着实不可思议。
t_en: When you wake up tomorrow morning, you'll see it. When you go to sleep tomorrow night, you'll see it... This is truly incredible.

t_ch: 困意逐渐袭来……
t_en: The drowsiness is encroaching...

t_ch: 睡觉。
t_en: Sleep.

t_ch: 晚安，星穹列车。
t_en: Goodnight, Astral Express.

t_ch: 我桌子上这是…<color=#dbc291ff>「星穹列车值日排班表」</color>？
t_en: What's this on my desk... <color=#dbc291ff>Astral Express Duty Roster</color>?

t_ch: 早哦{NICKNAME}乘客，昨天睡得好嘛？
t_en: Good morning, Passenger {NICKNAME}! Did you sleep well last night?

t_ch: 你是来给我送早餐的吗？
t_en: Did you come to bring me breakfast?

t_ch: 列车长大清早就来我的房间，一定是来给我送早餐的吧！
t_en: The Express conductor came to my room so early in the morning — must be to bring me breakfast!

t_ch: 你想得美！这都下午了帕！
t_en: Nice try! It's already the afternoon!

t_ch: 做了一个美梦。
t_en: I had a great dream.

t_ch: 梦见自己一夜之间有了好多「列车基金」，数不清也用不完，令人烦恼。
t_en: I dreamed that I suddenly had a huge amount of Express fund — so much that I couldn't count it all or even use it up. It was kind of overwhelming.

t_ch: 哦，那正好～帕姆正是来为你实现梦想的帕！
t_en: Oh, that's perfect~ Pom-Pom is here to help you make your dreams come true!

t_ch: 拿好你桌上这份「星穹列车值日排班表」～接下来，{NICKNAME}乘客要好好参加值日，赚取「列车基金」，把这个房间建设成自己想要的样子吧！
t_en: Take this Astral Express Duty Roster on your desk~ Next, Passenger {NICKNAME}, make sure to participate in the duties well, earn the Express fund, and turn this room into the way you want it!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，你准备好参与值日了吗？
t_en: Passenger {NICKNAME}, are you ready to start your shift?

t_ch: 我最喜欢值日了！
t_en: I love doing shifts!

t_ch: 很好，很有精神！
t_en: There we go. That's the spirit!

t_ch: 让我再休息一下…
t_en: Let me rest a bit longer...

t_ch: 唔…真拿你没办法！休息好了就快来哦，不许拖延和偷懒！帕姆会监督你的帕！
t_en: Alright, fine! Rest up and come quickly... No delaying or slacking off! Pom-Pom will be keeping an eye on you!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，你准备好参与值日了吗？
t_en: Passenger {NICKNAME}, are you ready to start your shift?

t_ch: 我最喜欢值日了！
t_en: I love doing shifts!

t_ch: 很好，很有精神！
t_en: There we go. That's the spirit!

t_ch: 让我再休息一下…
t_en: Let me rest a bit longer...

t_ch: 唔…真拿你没办法！休息好了就快来哦，不许拖延和偷懒！帕姆会监督你的帕！
t_en: Alright, fine! Rest up and come quickly... No delaying or slacking off! Pom-Pom will be keeping an eye on you!

t_ch: 这个吧台，一看就很有派对的氛围。
t_en: This counter has a party vibe to it.

t_ch: 再采购一些家具，就能邀请大伙来玩了。
t_en: Just a couple more pieces of furniture and we can invite everyone over to hang out.

t_ch: 玄关是住宅的门面，得装修得气派一些。
t_en: The entryway is the face of the home, so it should be decorated with a bit of flair.

t_ch: …等我有钱了，就雇两个全副武装的智械在这里站岗！
t_en: ...When I have the money, I'll hire two Intellitrons fully armed to the teeth to stand guard here!

t_ch: 人类不能失去电竞房，就像星际和平公司不能失去庇尔波因特。
t_en: Humans can't afford to lose the gaming room, just like the Interastral Peace Corporation can't afford to lose Pier Point.

t_ch: 早就准备好的装机清单，终于有了用武之地！
t_en: The build list I prepared long ago finally has a purpose!

t_ch: 这个收藏陈列间，规模上还是略逊于「黑塔」空间站的奇物收藏室……
t_en: This is a collectible showroom, though it's still somewhat smaller in scale compared to the Curio Collection Room of the Herta Space Station...

t_ch: 但总有一天，总有一天，黑塔也会震惊于我的收藏！哼！
t_en: But someday, someday, Herta will be astonished by my collection! Hmph!

t_ch: 好宽敞的浴室，好气派的浴缸！
t_en: What a spacious bathroom! What a grand bathtub!

t_ch: 我要在这里疯狂泡澡！我要成为浴缸深潜者！
t_en: I'm going to soak like crazy in here! I'm going to become a bathtub deep diver!

t_ch: 晚上好，亲爱的无名客。恭喜您完成了卧室的建造，看到这个房间恢复生机，我由衷地感到高兴。
t_en: Good evening, dear Nameless. Congratulations on finishing the bedroom construction! I'm genuinely thrilled to see this space come to life.

t_ch: 卧室之于无名客，如同能量核心之于智械。稳定的关机休眠，能让重启后的运行更加顺畅。
t_en: For the Nameless, the bedroom is like an energy core for the Intellitron. A stable shutdown and rest ensure smoother operation after rebooting.

t_ch: 而一个睡前故事，则如同关机前长按待机键般至关重要。
t_en: And a bedtime story is just as crucial as holding down the power button before shutting down.

t_ch: 听睡前故事。
t_en: Listen to a bedtime story.

t_ch: 那就讲讲吧。不过，千万别讲你那冷笑话了。
t_en: Alright, let's hear it. But please, no more of those bad jokes.

t_ch: 亲爱的无名客，您真会说笑。我何时讲过冷笑话？
t_en: Dear Nameless, you're quite the joker. When have I ever told bad jokes?

t_ch: 拒绝听睡前故事。
t_en: I'm not in the mood for a bedtime story.

t_ch: 要我听你那冷笑话，还不如让我先帮你长按待机键得了。
t_en: I'd rather hold down the power button for you than listen to one of your bad jokes.

t_ch: 这次绝对不冷！请您务必再给我一个机会！
t_en: I promise this one won't be bad! Please give me another chance!

t_ch: 一个智械上班族总是喜怒不定，时间久了，同事发现了此中规律：如果他很暴躁，昨晚一定是充了火电；如果他很平静，昨晚一定是充了水电。
t_en: An Intellitron always goes to work with different moods. Over time, his coworker noticed a pattern: If he's grumpy, he must have charged with heat-based energy the night before. If he's calm, he must have charged with hydropower.

t_ch: 有一天他上班时待人十分和气。提问：他充了什么电？
t_en: One day, he was unusually serene at work. Now, the question: What kind of energy did he charge with?

t_ch: 答案：是「和」电。您理解了吗？「和」与「核」谐音，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: Answer: He charged with nuclear power — because he's full of peace today! Geddit? Contrasting nuclear with peace, this tickles my funny bone.

t_ch: 我也给你讲一个。
t_en: Let me tell you one, too.

t_ch: 哦？亲爱的无名客，我早知道您也是一位幽默大师！我会将您的幽默牢牢存入语料库，传递给一代又一代无名客。
t_en: Oh? Dear Nameless, I always knew you were a master of humor! I'll make sure to save your jokes in the database and pass them on to future generations of the Nameless.

t_ch: 从前有个人，睡觉时喜欢用被子盖住鼻子和嘴巴，后来他一睡不起了。
t_en: Once, there was a person who liked to cover his nose and mouth with his blanket while sleeping. Eventually, no one saw him ever again.

t_ch: 提问：怎么会发生这样的惨剧呢？
t_en: Question: How did such a tragic event happen?

t_ch: 因为他没有「鼻嘴」。
t_en: Because he became a shut-in.

t_ch: 你理解了吗？「鼻嘴」和「闭嘴」谐音，表达了某种耐人寻味的警示，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: Do you get it? "Shut-in" sounds like "Shush," offering a thought-provoking warning, and makes me chuckle.

t_ch: 好的。请您好好休息，我这就滚。
t_en: Alright. Please get some good rest, I'll show myself out.

t_ch: （下一步，就装修浴室吧……）
t_en: (Next up, let's start decorating the bathroom...)

t_ch: （这么宽敞的浴室，一定要选个气派的浴缸！）
t_en: (With such a spacious bathroom, we definitely need to pick a grand bathtub!)

t_ch: （我要在这里疯狂泡澡！我要成为浴缸深潜者！）
t_en: (I'm going to soak like crazy in here! I'm going to become a bathtub deep diver!)

t_ch: 你的客厅可真宽敞，正适合开茶会。
t_en: Your living room is so spacious — perfect for hosting a tea party.

t_ch: 零食、饮料、水果，你准备的可真周到。不过我身为领航员，可不能只顾享受乘员的招待～
t_en: Snacks, drinks, fruit — you've thought of everything. But as the Navigator, I can't just enjoy the crew's hospitality...

t_ch: 正巧我前些日子刚买了新的咖啡豆，来前已经研磨完成。{NICKNAME}，我们一起尝尝看吧～
t_en: I actually just bought some new coffee beans recently, and I've already ground them. {NICKNAME}, let's give it a try together!

t_ch: 「哦，糟糕。」你脑中响起了一个声音，「姬子小姐制作的咖啡，口味可未必是你能承受的……」
t_en: "Oh, no." A voice in your head says, "Miss Himeko's coffee might not be to your taste..."

t_ch: 我喝果汁就可以了！
t_en: I'll just stick with juice!

t_ch: 你已经喝了4、5杯果汁了。这东西看似健康，但糖分很高的。黑咖啡没有糖分，还能改善消化系统环境，对身体很好的。喝一杯吧～
t_en: You've already had 4 or 5 glasses of juice. It may seem healthy, but it's actually high in sugar. Black coffee has no sugar and can improve your digestive system, making it good for your health. Have a cup!

t_ch: 我不想喝咖啡…
t_en: I don't want to drink coffee...

t_ch: 你的「勇气」值似乎还不足以让你说出这句话。
t_en: It seems like your "courage" isn't quite enough to let you say that.

t_ch: 你看黑咖啡没有糖分，还能改善消化系统环境，对身体很好的。喝一杯吧～
t_en: Black coffee has no sugar and can benefit your digestive system. It's good for your health. How about having a cup?

t_ch: 沉默间，姬子已经泡好一杯「黑咖啡」，递到你的手中。你凝望着残渣漂浮的漆黑咖啡，它也凝望着你。
t_en: In the silence, Himeko has already brewed a cup of "black coffee" and handed it to you. You gaze at the dark coffee with floating grounds, and it seems to gaze back at you.

t_ch: 泡咖啡的一大乐趣在于，咖啡豆的品种、颗粒研磨的粗细、水温和冲泡步骤…每一点不同，都能带来全新的口感。很像我们的旅途，不是吗？
t_en: One of the great pleasures of brewing coffee is that each variation — whether it's the type of coffee beans, the grind size, the water temperature, or the brewing method — can bring a completely new flavor. It's much like our journey, isn't it?

t_ch: 做出的每个「选择」，都将使我们奔赴一个难以预料的「结果」。
t_en: Every "choice" we make leads us to an unpredictable "outcome."

t_ch: 「咖啡」是这么随机的产物吗？
t_en: Is coffee really such a random product?

t_ch: 也许有些人每次泡咖啡都能抵达既定的结果，但这不是我喜欢的方式。每次踏上旅程，都应有些新发现，不是吗？
t_en: Some people might achieve the same result every time they brew coffee, but that's not how I like to do it. Every journey should bring some new discoveries, don't you think?

t_ch: 长痛不如短痛，干杯！
t_en: Better a quick pain than a long one. Cheers!

t_ch: 慢点喝，小心呛到。
t_en: Take your time drinking it — be careful not to choke.

t_ch: 你闭上眼，一饮而尽。这杯咖啡的味道……
t_en: You close your eyes and take a deep sip. The taste of this coffee...

t_ch: 浓郁的芬芳自口腔冲入鼻腔，油脂般醇厚的口感配合着恰到好处的酸苦，激活了你的每一个细胞。
t_en: The rich aroma flows from your mouth to your nose, and the creamy, full-bodied taste paired with just the right amount of acidity and bitterness invigorates every cell in your body.

t_ch: 这真是一杯美味的咖啡。
t_en: This is truly a delicious cup of coffee.

t_ch: 你看，不踏上旅程，就永远不会知道自己能有什么收获。
t_en: See, if you don't embark on the journey, you'll never know what you might gain.

t_ch: （下一步，就装修玄关吧……）
t_en: (Next, let's decorate the entryway...)

t_ch: （玄关是住宅的门面，得装修得气派一些。）
t_en: (The entryway is the face of the home, so it should be decorated with a bit of flair.)

t_ch: （…等我有钱了，就雇两个全副武装的智械在这里站岗！）
t_en: (...When I have the money, I'll hire two Intellitrons fully armed to the teeth to stand guard here!)

t_ch: 准备好了吗，{NICKNAME}乘客…无比激动人心的「卫生大评比」！
t_en: Ready, Passenger {NICKNAME}? The incredibly thrilling Hygiene Showdown is here!

t_ch: 为了守护列车的环境，每隔一段时间列车长都会对无名客们的房间进行卫生评比。
t_en: To maintain the cleanliness of the Express, Pom-Pom periodically conducts hygiene inspections of the passengers' rooms.

t_ch: 评比最高分，可以获得「卫生之星」的称号！顺带一提，丹恒乘客已经蝉联三届「卫生之星」了帕。
t_en: The highest score will receive the title of Hygiene Star! By the way, Dan Heng has been the reigning Hygiene Star for three consecutive years!

t_ch: 不公平！丹恒的家具少！
t_en: Not fair! Dan Heng has fewer furnishings!

t_ch: 明白了，那列车长就把你的家具全部没收帕！
t_en: Got it. Then Pom-Pom will confiscate all your furniture!

t_ch: 「卫生之星」有什么奖励吗？
t_en: Is there a prize for being a Hygiene Star?

t_ch: 会得到帕姆的表扬～至于实物奖励嘛…「闭嘴」建议提供一个「卫生纸星」作为奖章，但帕姆猜你们不会喜欢的帕。
t_en: You'll receive a commendation from me~ As for a tangible reward... Shush suggests a Toilet Paper Star as a medal, but Pom-Pom is guessing you might not like that.

t_ch: 我再收拾收拾…
t_en: I'll tidy up a bit more...

t_ch: 一周前就已经通知过了，现在临时抱佛脚，为时已晚了帕！
t_en: You were notified a week ago. It's too late to scramble at the last minute now!

t_ch: 放心，{NICKNAME}乘客。帕姆明白，你这里还在装修，肯定不会太整洁。列车长会充分考虑目前的情形，做出公正的评判。
t_en: Don't worry, Passenger {NICKNAME}. Pom-Pom understands that you're still renovating, so it might not be very tidy. I will take the current situation into account and make a fair assessment.

t_ch: 清洁度，九分；整洁度，八分；美观度……
t_en: Cleanliness: 9. Tidiness: 8. Aesthetics...

t_ch: 这棵盆栽，叶子翠绿，质地饱满，体态匀称，实在是前所未见…{NICKNAME}乘客，可以告诉帕姆是怎么做到的吗？
t_en: This potted plant has vibrant green leaves, a full texture, and a perfectly balanced shape — truly exceptional... Passenger {NICKNAME}, could you share with Pom-Pom how you achieved this?

t_ch: 不打扰干涉，让它自由生长。
t_en: I don't interfere, just let it grow freely.

t_ch: 用「爱」浇灌。
t_en: Water it with love.

t_ch: 很好的回答！美观度，一百分！
t_en: Excellent answer! Aesthetics: <unbreak>100</unbreak>!

t_ch: 原来是百分制吗？！
t_en: So it's a percentage scale!?

t_ch: （下一步，就装修电竞房吧……）
t_en: (Next, let's decorate the gaming room...)

t_ch: （人类不能失去电竞房，就像星际和平公司不能失去庇尔波因特。）
t_en: (Humans can't afford to lose the gaming room, just like the Interastral Peace Corporation can't afford to lose Pier Point.)

t_ch: （早就准备好的装机清单，终于有了用武之地！）
t_en: The build list I prepared long ago finally has a purpose!

t_ch: 哼哼，{NICKNAME}乘客，有没有发现帕姆今天有什么不同？
t_en: Hmm, Passenger {NICKNAME}, have you noticed anything different about Pom-Pom today?

t_ch: 气色变好了？
t_en: Looking better?

t_ch: 穿上了新衣服，可不衬托得列车长我气色更好嘛～
t_en: Pom-Pom is wearing new clothes — don't they make Pom-Pom look even better?

t_ch: 换上了新衣服？
t_en: Did you change into new clothes?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客果然敏锐。帕姆的新衣服很不错帕！
t_en: You're as sharp as ever, Passenger {NICKNAME}. Pom-Pom's new outfit looks great!

t_ch: 你不是真正的帕姆！
t_en: You're not the real Pom-Pom!

t_ch: 换了新衣服，{NICKNAME}乘客就认不出我了吗！本列车长可是如假包换的列车长帕！
t_en: Pom-Pom changed into new clothes, and now you don't recognize me, Passenger {NICKNAME}? Pom-Pom is the real deal, authentic through and through!

t_ch: 这套压箱底的厨师套装，偶尔也会翻出来， 应对一些重大事务，比如现在——
t_en: This top-tier chef's outfit, which Pom-Pom occasionally brings out for special occasions, is just what Pom-Pom needs for something important like this...

t_ch: 显示屏可以打亮，程序也运行正常，问题解决了。
t_en: The display can now turn on, and the program is running smoothly. The issue is resolved.

t_ch: 你只不过是把连接线插错了插槽，漏过了两个卡扣，装反了元胞运算芯片。都是些小问题，作为一个新手，能做到这一步已经不错。
t_en: You just plugged the cables into the wrong ports, missed a couple of clips, and installed the cellular calculation chip in reverse. These are minor issues. For a beginner, you've done quite well.

t_ch: 不愧是杨叔！
t_en: Just as expected from Mr. Yang!

t_ch: 只要「理解」其中原理，组装机箱就并不困难。
t_en: As long as you understand the underlying principles, assembling the case isn't difficult.

t_ch: 不愧是我！
t_en: I never fail to impress!

t_ch: 哈哈，再锻炼几回，下次就可以你来维修列车了。
t_en: Ha ha, with a bit more practice, next time you could handle the Express repairs yourself.

t_ch: 不过我还是略感震惊。元胞运算芯片的插槽上有防呆设计，理论上反过来是根本插不进去的。生产商大概也没料到，你真是有一身使不完的力气……
t_en: I'm still a bit surprised. The cellular calculation chip has a foolproof design, so it theoretically shouldn't fit if inserted incorrectly. The manufacturer probably didn't anticipate this. You really have a remarkable amount of strength...

t_ch: 虽说现在已经有了不少先进的显示技术，全息、幻造、脑机接口…但我还是更喜欢实实在在的显示屏。哈哈，也许是我不够时髦了。
t_en: Even though there are many advanced display technologies now — holograms, illusions, brain-computer interfaces — I still prefer a good old-fashioned screen. Haha, maybe I'm just not trendy enough.

t_ch: 我年轻时，也会比较各种硬件的参数和价格，自己组装计算机，以及一些…更庞大的机器。
t_en: When I was younger, I used to compare the specifications and prices of various hardware, assemble computers myself, and even work on some... much larger machines.

t_ch: 拟真幻彩显示屏、拟神经电子脑处理器…哼，真是豪华啊。你打算用它做什么呢？
t_en: Realistic holographic display screens, neuro-synthetic electronic brain processors... Hmm, that's quite luxurious. What do you plan to use them for?

t_ch: 用来看剧。
t_en: To watch shows.

t_ch: 我房间里收藏了不少好看的动画，如果你想看，可以随时向我借。
t_en: I have a collection of great animations in my room. If you want to watch them, feel free to borrow them anytime.

t_ch: 用来打游戏。
t_en: To play games.

t_ch: 如果玩多人竞技游戏时缺队友，可以随时联系我。你的杨叔可是宝刀未老呢。
t_en: If you ever need teammates for multiplayer games, feel free to contact me. I'm still going strong!

t_ch: 用来成为最强的骇客！
t_en: To become the ultimate hacker!

t_ch: 我不太懂这方面的技术，但也许可以给你推荐一些入门教材。期待你学成的那天。
t_en: I'm not very familiar with this kind of technology, but I can recommend some beginner's materials to you. Looking forward to the day you master it.

t_ch: （下一步，就装修收藏陈列间吧……）
t_en: (Next up, let's renovate the display room for the collection...)

t_ch: （虽然规模上还是略逊于「黑塔」空间站的奇物收藏室……）
t_en: Although it's still somewhat smaller in scale compared to the Curio Collection Room of the Herta Space Station...

t_ch: （但总有一天，总有一天，黑塔也会震惊于我的收藏！哼！）
t_en: But someday, someday, Herta will be astonished by my collection! Hmph!

t_ch: 这收藏间不算小，能摆上不少纪念品。但如果没有随时保持规整的好习惯，即便收藏空间再大，也会在不知不觉间被慢慢堆满。
t_en: The collection room isn't small. It can hold a lot of memorabilia. But without the habit of keeping it tidy, even the largest space will gradually fill up with clutter.

t_ch: 教教我，丹恒老师！
t_en: Teach me, Master Dan Heng!

t_ch: 丹恒老师，蝉联三届的列车「卫生之星」！今天请你过来，就是为了向你请教收纳的秘诀！
t_en: Master Dan Heng, three-time reigning Hygiene Star of the Express! I asked you here today to learn your secrets for organization!

t_ch: 收纳的根本问题，在于如何分门别类。这和做科学研究倒是一脉相承。
t_en: The fundamental issue with organization is how to categorize things. It's quite similar to conducting scientific research.

t_ch: 就像「星空生态学派」主张根据特征的相似和演化，绘制宇宙生命演化的总表图。而「烛墨学派」则主张按照首字母，将一切知识编入百科全书。
t_en: Just like how the Astral Ecology School advocates creating a comprehensive chart of the evolution of cosmic life based on similarities and evolution of characteristics. In contrast, the Candelagraphos advocates organizing all knowledge into an encyclopedia according to the initial letters.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你更喜欢哪一种？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, which one do you prefer?

t_ch: 依据特征。
t_en: Based on characteristics.

t_ch: 摆件的归摆件，海报的归海报！
t_en: Figurines belong with figurines, posters belong with posters!

t_ch: 我在整理智库条目时，也是如此。
t_en: It's the same when I'm organizing entries in the data bank.

t_ch: 依据首字母。
t_en: By alphabetical order.

t_ch: 要是现实世界也有一键排序就好了。
t_en: It would be great if the real world had a one-click sorting feature.

t_ch: 我在整理藏书时，也是如此。
t_en: I use the same method when organizing my collection of books.

t_ch: 依据心情。
t_en: Based on mood.

t_ch: 我是「随心所欲学派」。
t_en: I'm from the Go-With-the-Flow School.

t_ch: 很符合我对你的想象。
t_en: That fits perfectly with what I imagined about you.

t_ch: 不过我倒是觉得，如果这个收藏间变得过于「井然有序」，反倒失去了一些生活感，而变得像是不动产商的样板间了。
t_en: However, I think if this collection room becomes too neat and orderly, it might lose some of its lived-in feel and start to resemble a showroom from a real estate agent.

t_ch: 也许不必那么严格，随心所欲一些就好。无名客的生活总是充满疲惫，在这种小事上，不妨就放过自己吧。
t_en: Perhaps it doesn't need to be so strict. A bit more spontaneity is fine. The lives of the Nameless are always full of fatigue, so it's okay to cut yourself some slack on these small matters.

t_ch: 空间宽敞、照明舒适，再加上这个足够让帕姆当游泳池的浴缸……
t_en: With the spacious room, comfortable lighting, and this bathtub that's big enough for Pom-Pom to use as a swimming pool...

t_ch: 你的浴室，我可以打九十分！
t_en: I'd give your bathroom a score of <unbreak>90</unbreak>!

t_ch: 感谢三月七小姐的测评！
t_en: Thank you, Miss March 7th, for the review!

t_ch: 为了恭喜浴室落成，送给你这个「巴门尼德入浴剂」！
t_en: To celebrate the completion of the bathroom, I'm giving you this Parmenides Bath Soak!

t_ch: 剩下十分扣在了哪儿？
t_en: Where did the remaining ten points get deducted?

t_ch: 哼哼，从泡澡爱好者的专业角度来看，还缺少一个最重要的东西。
t_en: Hmph, from the perspective of a bath enthusiast, it's still missing the most important thing.

t_ch: 那就是——入浴剂！这个「巴门尼德入浴剂」就送给你啦。
t_en: And that is — Bath Soak! I'll give you this Parmenides Bath Soak.

t_ch: 这可是「博识学会」和「技术研发部」通力合作推出的重磅产品呢！据说还有位著名的天才教授参与了研发！据说用了这个入浴剂，泡完澡会变聪明！
t_en: This is a major product jointly developed by the Intelligentsia Guild and the IPC's Technology Department! It is said that a renowned genius professor was involved in its development! Supposedly, using this bath soak will make you smarter after a bath!

t_ch: 它提取了翁瓦克红树林第一批嫩芽的精粹，在泡澡的时候，香味混合在氤氲的蒸汽里，让你好像置身热带雨林的山涧泉水，倾听鸟儿的婉转啼鸣……
t_en: It extracts the essence from the first batch of tender shoots from the Vonwacq mangroves. During your bath, the fragrance blends with the steamy mist, making you feel as if you're in a tropical rainforest, listening to the melodious songs of the birds...

t_ch: 至少广告上是这么说的，而且两份起购，还打六折优惠哦。
t_en: At least that's what the advertisement says. And if you buy two, you'll get a <unbreak>40%</unbreak> discount!

t_ch: 你被公司忽悠了！
t_en: You've been deceived by the IPC!

t_ch: 诶诶诶？原来只是广告推销的说辞吗！
t_en: Eh? So it's just an advertising pitch!

t_ch: 这就是多余的那份吧？
t_en: So, this must be the extra one, right?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，有谁能拒绝六折优惠呢。四舍五入一下，相当于第二份不要钱嘛。
t_en: Hehe, who can resist a <unbreak>40%</unbreak> discount? Rounded up, it's like getting the second one for free!

t_ch: 那位教授真的会参与研发吗？
t_en: Will that professor really be involved in the development?

t_ch: 这谁知道？反正广告上是这么说的！
t_en: Who knows? Anyway, that's what the advertisement says!

t_ch: 除此之外，这个「浴室里的星际旅行」系列，我还买了很多热门好物呢！
t_en: Besides this, I also bought many popular items from the Interstellar Travel series for the bathroom!

t_ch: 百分百可以折射出彩虹的花洒，附带湿度显示功能的防水音响，还有贾勒巴岩浆岩提取的浴盐，用了之后池水会像岩浆一样咕嘟咕嘟冒泡……
t_en: A shower head that can reflect rainbows <unbreak>100%</unbreak> of the time, a waterproof speaker with a built-in humidity display, and bath salts extracted from Jalleba lava rock that make the bathwater bubble like magma...

t_ch: 看来这入浴剂没有让你变聪明。
t_en: It looks like this bath soak didn't make you any smarter.

t_ch: 嘿～竟敢讽刺本姑娘！
t_en: Hey~ Tease me again, I dare you!

t_ch: 你把头伸过来，我给你增加点智慧！
t_en: Come closer, and I'll add a bit of wisdom for you!

t_ch: 下次购物记得叫上我。
t_en: Remember to call me next time you go shopping.

t_ch: 没问题～星际和平商城的优惠狂欢季可吓人了，咱俩得携手杀出一条血路！
t_en: No problem! The discounts and deals at the Interastral Peace Mall are crazy. We'll need to team up and fight our way through!

t_ch: （下一步，就装修客厅吧……）
t_en: (Next step, let's decorate the living room...)

t_ch: （客厅里的家具，得早早置办起来！）
t_en: (We need to get the furniture for the living room sorted out soon!)

t_ch: （再采购一些零食和饮料，就能邀请大伙来玩了。）
t_en: (Once we buy some snacks and drinks, we can invite everyone over to hang out.)

t_ch: 再次祝您今夜好梦，我这就告退。
t_en: Once again, I wish you sweet dreams tonight. I'll take my leave now.

t_ch: 我的购物车里还有好多居家热门好物呢，需要的话，我分享链接给你~
t_en: I have lots of popular home items in my shopping cart. If you need, I can share the links with you~

t_ch: 每一次冲泡咖啡都能带来全新的口感…很像我们的旅途，不是吗？
t_en: Each time you brew coffee, it brings a whole new flavor... It's very much like our journey, don't you think?

t_ch: 帕姆正在计算本次「卫生大评比」的总分…敬请期待评比结果帕！
t_en: I'm calculating the total score for this Hygiene Showdown... Stay tuned for the results!

t_ch: 拟真幻彩显示屏、拟神经电子脑处理器…真是豪华啊。就连我也想要置办一套了。
t_en: Realistic holographic display screens, neuro-synthetic electronic brain processors... It's truly luxurious. Even I want to get a set for myself.

t_ch: 藏品的摆放还是随心所欲一些为好。无名客的生活总是充满疲惫，在这种小事上，不妨就放过自己吧。
t_en: Arranging collectibles is best done as you please. A Nameless's life is always full of fatigue, so why not give yourself a break on these small matters?

t_ch: 该开始工作啦！
t_en: It's time to start work!

t_ch: 哎哟，我也想翻新一遍自己的房间了。
t_en: Wow, this makes me feel like revamping my own room.

t_ch: 看着这里从无到有逐渐充实起来，真让人舒心。这种好日子，就该做些什么来庆祝一下！
t_en: It's so nice seeing this empty space gradually fill up with furniture. A happy occasion like this calls for a celebration!

t_ch: 我们不是正在庆祝么？
t_en: Aren't we already celebrating?

t_ch: 不是啦！是说{NICKNAME}自己一个人的庆祝！
t_en: No, silly! I'm talking about {NICKNAME} celebrating in {M#his}{F#her} own way!

t_ch: 换作是我，肯定要在晚上好好泡个热水澡，尽情享受属于自己的时间～
t_en: If it were me, I'd take a relaxing hot bath in the evening, and enjoy some me time.

t_ch: 真是独特的庆祝方式！
t_en: That's a unique way of celebrating.

t_ch: 哼哼，本姑娘可是「星穹列车泡澡协会」的创始人！
t_en: Hehe, you're talking to the founder of the Astral Express Bath Association, after all!

t_ch: 你房间哪里有浴缸？
t_en: Is there even a bathtub in your room?

t_ch: 隐藏式设计，你不懂了吧？
t_en: It's concealed, of course! Ever heard of secret rooms?

t_ch: 确实，也是时候让{NICKNAME}享受一下个人时间了。
t_en: All I know is, {NICKNAME} deserves to take some time off for {M#himself}{F#herself}.

t_ch: 虽然房间建好了，但以后也要好好值日帕！列车也该启程了，要准备的事情还非常多呢！
t_en: Even though your room is set up, you must remember to keep up with your duties! The Express will be departing soon and there's still so many things to do!

t_ch: 那么，{NICKNAME}，再次祝你——
t_en: And now, {NICKNAME}, we'd once again like to say...

t_ch: 入住快乐！！！
t_en: Congrats on the new home!

t_ch: 大家碰杯，饮尽杯中的饮料，随后欢笑着告辞，一起离开了你的房间。
t_en: The group raises their glasses, drinking in unison, then with laughter, they bid farewell and exit your room together.

t_ch: 随后，几乎房门关上的下个瞬间，又有人敲响了你的房门。
t_en: Just as the door is about to fully close, there's another knock at your door.

t_ch: 亲爱的无名客，您现在有空吗？
t_en: Dear Nameless, are you available right now?

t_ch: 亲爱的无名客，晚上好。
t_en: Good evening, my Nameless friend.

t_ch: 需要些饮料吗？或是一些茶点？我的知识库显示，夜宵或许会对健康造成负担，却能带来超乎想象的快乐。
t_en: Would you like anything to drink? How about some refreshments? My knowledge base indicates that while late night snacks may negatively affect one's health, they can also bring great joy.

t_ch: 我认识一位年轻的无名客，他早睡早起，坚持锻炼，严格规划热量摄取，拒绝了我的每一条建议。至于夜宵…在他眼中简直是毒药。
t_en: I once knew a young Nameless who was both early to bed and early to rise. He had a regular exercise routine and strictly regulated his caloric intake, rejecting every suggestion that I ever gave him. To him, midnight snacks were no different from poison.

t_ch: 后来，他度过了健康、长寿的一生。
t_en: As a result, he lived a long and healthy life.

t_ch: 亲爱的无名客，这是个令人警醒的案例，不是吗？
t_en: It's quite a tale of caution, is it not, dear Nameless?

t_ch: 太棒了，我就想吃宵夜！
t_en: Cool story, but I just want supper right now!

t_ch: 对不起，此地是一片没有「宵夜」的战场。这个笑话怎么样？「宵夜」与「硝烟」谐音，令人忍俊不禁。
t_en: Ah, but you shouldn't have supper if you want to be super. How did you find the joke? Hilarious wordplay, isn't it? You can't help but laugh, can you?

t_ch: 更想要长寿的一生。
t_en: I'd rather live a long life.

t_ch: 已录入{NICKNAME}乘客的饮食习惯数据，从明日起将为您调整食谱，提供营养更加均衡的餐食。明日午餐：萝卜丁、生菜叶、鸡胸肉，淋柠檬汁。
t_en: {NICKNAME}'s dietary preferences have been saved. Starting tomorrow, your meals will be changed to a more nutritionally balanced menu. Tomorrow's lunch menu includes diced carrots, raw lettuce, and chicken breast with lemon drizzle.

t_ch: 给我调回去！
t_en: Change it back!

t_ch: 言归正传。听说您有泡澡的打算，我决定前来送您一份恰当的礼物，一块有着传奇历史的香皂。
t_en: Ah, but I digress. After hearing about your plans for a good soak, I thought this would make a most suitable gift and wanted you to have it. It's a bar of soap with a storied history.

t_ch: 这是阿斯德纳的狼人英雄「哈努努」生前最爱用的香皂。
t_en: Hanunue, the werewolf hero of Asdana, loved using this soap when he was alive.

t_ch: 「闭嘴」的半球形玻璃罩缓缓打开，机械臂将升降台上的「香皂」取出，郑重地放进你的手里。
t_en: The hemispherical glass dome of "Shush" slowly opens. A robotic arm carefully retrieves the "soap" from the platform and solemnly places it into your hands.

t_ch: 泛着银光的底板已经略带锈迹，根根挺立的铁丝却仍然扎手。
t_en: The silvery surface of the base has already started to show signs of rust, but the upright iron bristles are still sharp to the touch.

t_ch: 可能是习俗不同吧，在你那里类似的事物一般叫「金属毛刷」。
t_en: It might be a difference in customs, but where you are from, this kind of item is usually called a "metal brush."

t_ch: 这玩意叫「香皂」？？？
t_en: This thingamajig is a bar of soap?

t_ch: 狼人的毛发不好打理。
t_en: Werewolf fur is a pain to manage, after all.

t_ch: 忙碌了一天的无名客啊，请问，您要先洗澡、先喝杯饮料、还是要先听我再讲个笑话呢？
t_en: It's been a long and busy day for you, Nameless. Would you like to take a bath, enjoy a beverage, or listen to another of my jokes first?

t_ch: 我要洗澡了，请你出去。
t_en: I'm gonna take a bath. Please leave.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，你来得正好！帕姆最近添了些新衣服，决定在这里给自己安排一个衣帽间～
t_en: Passenger {NICKNAME}, you arrived just in time! Pom-Pom recently got some new clothes and decided to set up a coatroom here for myself~

t_ch: 你看看，这个位置怎么样？
t_en: What do you think of this spot?

t_ch: 非常合适！
t_en: Perfect!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，帕姆也这么觉得。刚好塞满了这个角落！
t_en: Hehe, Pom-Pom think so too. It fits into this corner perfectly!

t_ch: 有别的主意。
t_en: I have other ideas.

t_ch: 哦？关于帕姆衣帽间的位置，{NICKNAME}乘客有更好的想法吗？
t_en: Oh? Do you have a better idea for Pom-Pom's Coatroom, Passenger {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 放在观景车厢。
t_en: How about placing it in the Parlor Car?

t_ch: 怎么能放在观景车厢？难道「帕姆换装」是什么景观吗？
t_en: How can it be placed in the Parlor Car? Is my changing clothes a sightseeing attraction now?

t_ch: 放在资料室。
t_en: How about placing it in the Archives?

t_ch: 那不就变成丹恒乘客的衣帽间了吗？
t_en: Wouldn't that make it Dan Heng's coatroom instead?

t_ch: 放在货运车厢。
t_en: How about placing it in the cargo carriage?

t_ch: 别开玩笑了帕！帕姆对生活品质要求很高的！
t_en: Stop joking around! Pom-Pom has high standards for quality of life!

t_ch: 暂时驳回！目前看来，还是这个地方最合适。
t_en: Rejected for now! For the moment, this spot still seems the most suitable.

t_ch: 不能放在你自己的房间吗？
t_en: Can't it be placed in your own room?

t_ch: 哼哼，帕姆可是列车长～整个列车都是帕姆自己的房间！
t_en: Hmph, Pom-Pom is the conductor~ The entire train is Pom-Pom's room!

t_ch: 那么，衣帽间的位置就决定是这儿啦。{NICKNAME}乘客，来都来了，顺带帮帕姆看看今天穿什么帕！
t_en: Then, the coatroom placement is decided! Passenger {NICKNAME}, since you're here, help Pom-Pom decide what to wear today!

t_ch: 听说你正在筹备什么计划？
t_en: I heard that you're currently working on some kind of plan?

t_ch: 你从哪儿听…咳咳，我是说，没错！
t_en: Where did you... Ahem... I mean, you're right!

t_ch: 我，达米亚诺斯，发现了一个绝佳的商机。而它就躺在圣城的围墙外面。
t_en: I, Damionis, have discovered a superb business opportunity lying just outside the walls of the holy city.

t_ch: 你想入伙吗，英雄？只要得手，我们就会成为新时代的开拓者。保准赚个盆满钵满！
t_en: Do you want to join in, hero? If we get our hands on it, we will surely become the trailblazers of the new era! Great fortune is awaiting us!

t_ch: 上回你可把我害惨了！
t_en: Hmph, you got me in hot soup last time!

t_ch: 嗐，我那不是…太激动嘛。这次肯定不会出乱子——探险可是我的老本行！
t_en: Aye, can you really blame me...? I was just too excited back then. But no one's getting into hot soup this time, because adventuring is what I do!

t_ch: 你居然会喜欢钱？
t_en: You're actually interested in making money?
